noteP1: We picked fifteen companies from our portfolio that map to the priorities of Live Nation Entertainment's technology organization: frontier AI and the infrastructure to run it economically, the data foundations underneath, and the security and developer tooling that let both move to production.
noteP2: Each card opens to a brief and the angle we think fits Live Nation Entertainment best, and you can filter by category. Our full portfolio of 150+ active companies is linked in the top right. Whenever it's convenient, send back the ones worth a closer look.
portfolioIntro: Every company here works at the infrastructure layer, across AI, data, security, and developer tooling, with an angle on where it fits Live Nation Entertainment's agenda. Filter by category, then expand any card for the brief.

anthropic.pull: Live Nation is operating a global live-events and ticketing platform that must absorb extreme demand peaks while strengthening identity, fraud defenses, venue technology and cyber resilience. The hardest work is often buried in ticket inventory, orders, fan profiles, event schedules, venue data, artist content and fraud signals and the systems that surround them. Anthropic matters because Claude can handle long, interdependent context, support agentic workflows and give developers Claude Code inside the same enterprise AI strategy. That creates a practical bridge between ticketing engineers, venue teams, promoters, fraud analysts, customer-care staff and security teams and the teams maintaining ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications. Deployed with explicit human checkpoints and evaluation, it can advance reliable fan and venue experiences during the moments of highest demand while keeping availability, fraud, privacy, consumer fairness and breach exposure visible rather than implicit.
cases: Reason across ticket inventory, orders, fan profiles, event schedules, venue data, artist content and fraud signals without forcing teams into a separate knowledge system.
Use Claude Code across ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications and the integration services behind them.
Keep human review, model policy and audit evidence explicit in high-consequence workflows.

wispr.pull: The information gap at Live Nation is not always a missing database; often it is what ticketing engineers, venue teams, promoters, fraud analysts, customer-care staff and security teams know but do not record during venue reports, promoter calls, incident notes, fan-service conversations and production observations. Wispr Flow lets users speak naturally into existing desktop and mobile tools, producing structured, edited text with commands and filler-word cleanup. That makes it well suited to distributed or frontline work around event creation, onsale, entry, settlement and fan-support workflows. The technology organization gains more timely operational context without asking teams to adopt a separate note-taking system or spend the end of a shift reconstructing what happened.
cases: Turn venue reports, promoter calls, incident notes, fan-service conversations and production observations into structured entries while the context is still fresh.
Work inside ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications rather than creating another application to manage.
Create a richer decision trail for handoffs, analytics and supervised agents.

browserbase.pull: The API surface around Live Nation does not cover every workflow. Teams still depend on venue portals, artist tools, partner sites, payment consoles and legacy operational systems, creating manual handoffs and fragile scripts. Browserbase gives agents a managed browser layer that can navigate those tools, preserve session context and produce an observable execution trail. It would be useful both for automating bounded tasks and for continuously testing ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications across real browser conditions. For the technology organization, that means extending automation into the web estate while maintaining governance around credentials, data access and availability, fraud, privacy, consumer fairness and breach exposure.
cases: Automate bounded tasks across venue portals, artist tools, partner sites, payment consoles and legacy operational systems without waiting for every system to expose an API.
Run repeatable browser tests across ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications before customer- or employee-facing releases.
Centralize sessions, credentials and execution logs for governed agent activity.

coder.pull: Developer productivity at Live Nation is constrained when setup, hardware and local configuration differ across the teams building ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications. Coder replaces that variability with centrally configured cloud development environments that can be provisioned from policy and shut down when not in use. The model is relevant to availability, fraud, privacy, consumer fairness and breach exposure because code, credentials and regulated tools remain inside the enterprise boundary. It also gives new engineers and acquired or distributed teams a consistent starting point, shortening the path from access request to a productive, compliant workspace.
cases: Standardize cloud workspaces for teams building ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications.
Keep source code, credentials and approved AI tooling inside controlled infrastructure.
Reduce onboarding and environment drift across distributed, acquired or specialized teams.

token.pull: The growth of ticketing services, partner APIs, venue devices, automation bots and AI agents across highly elastic ticketing and event workloads across cloud and venue environments creates an ownership and least-privilege problem that traditional workforce IAM does not fully cover. Token Security matters because it is purpose-built to discover, govern and audit non-human and AI-agent identities across their lifecycle. For Live Nation, that would make it possible to see which machine identity can reach fan, event, ticket, inventory, payment, venue and fraud data, who owns it and whether access still matches business intent. Live Nation's current agenda—operating a global live-events and ticketing platform that must absorb extreme demand peaks while strengthening identity, fraud defenses, venue technology and cyber resilience—will increase the number of autonomous credentials operating inside a high-consequence environment.
cases: Inventory ticketing services, partner APIs, venue devices, automation bots and AI agents and tie every credential to an accountable owner.
Constrain agent access to the specific fan, event, ticket, inventory, payment, venue and fraud data required for each workflow.
Preserve lifecycle and entitlement evidence for audits, regulators and internal risk review.

fal.pull: Live Nation has practical multimodal needs across concert video, artist media, venue imagery, fan content and event marketing assets. fal matters because it gives product and engineering teams a high-speed inference layer for image, video, audio and voice models through simple APIs, without forcing each use case to become an infrastructure project. The technology organization could use it to prototype and scale bounded generative experiences alongside ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications, from content production to simulation or visual assistance. The strongest fit is where latency and iteration speed matter but the risk profile around availability, fraud, privacy, consumer fairness and breach exposure still requires approved models, controlled inputs and a clear review path.
cases: Embed image, video, audio or voice generation into new ticketing, venue, fan, artist and event-operations capabilities.
Prototype against multiple media models through one developer-facing inference layer.
Keep review, consent and brand or safety controls in the surrounding application workflow.

montecarlo.pull: Live Nation's AI and operating decisions increasingly depend on fan, event, ticket, inventory, payment, venue and fraud data. A broken pipeline, silent schema change or weak model input can therefore surface as a service, safety or operational problem rather than a data-team ticket. Monte Carlo matters because it unifies observability across data pipelines, systems, code, models and the agents built on top. The technology organization would gain a common view of lineage, incidents and downstream impact across customer service, fraud detection, demand forecasting, personalization and event-operations agents. That trust layer is central to reliable fan and venue experiences during the moments of highest demand, especially where early detection and clear ownership are essential to managing availability, fraud, privacy, consumer fairness and breach exposure.
cases: Monitor fan, event, ticket, inventory, payment, venue and fraud data from source through dashboards, models and agent actions.
Trace incidents to the affected ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications and accountable engineering teams.
Prevent silent data failures from undermining reliable fan and venue experiences during the moments of highest demand.

tonic.pull: Live Nation's data is valuable precisely because it reflects real event creation, onsale, entry, settlement and fan-support workflows; it is also too sensitive to copy casually into test, analytics or model-development environments. Tonic creates a privacy-preserving alternative by synthesizing and de-identifying fan, ticket, payment, identity and event data while maintaining useful relationships and distributions. The technology organization could give teams working on ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications faster access to representative datasets, including rare scenarios that are difficult to find safely in production. It advances reliable fan and venue experiences during the moments of highest demand while avoiding the permanent trade-off between useful data access and privacy.
cases: Replace production copies of fan, ticket, payment, identity and event data in development and test environments.
Generate rare and edge-case scenarios for ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications and AI evaluation.
Reduce privacy exposure while giving teams faster access to useful data.

netbox.pull: Live Nation is a high-volume consumer platform spanning Ticketmaster, venues, promoters, artists, partners and regulated payment flows. That scale creates a persistent infrastructure-data problem: asset records, topology and configuration intent are often split across tools and local teams. NetBox Labs can centralize that context for venues, offices, cloud platforms, ticketing infrastructure and partner-connected networks and expose it programmatically to operations and automation. That is useful for everyday network changes, but also for the agentic future—the safest infrastructure agent is one grounded in an authoritative model of devices, sites, addresses and dependencies. For Live Nation, that supports faster change with less operational ambiguity.
cases: Create a maintained source of truth across venues, offices, cloud platforms, ticketing infrastructure and partner-connected networks.
Drive provisioning and change validation for highly elastic ticketing and event workloads across cloud and venue environments from intended state.
Ground infrastructure automation and agents in accurate topology and ownership data.

valar.pull: Live Nation is moving customer service, fraud detection, demand forecasting, personalization and event-operations agents toward production, where throughput, cost and data boundaries become architecture decisions rather than model-selection details. Valar matters because it runs high-volume async, batch and agentic inference inside the customer's own VPC. The platform can optimize scheduling, batching, routing and accelerator placement while prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies remain inside Live Nation's environment. That is especially relevant to availability, fraud, privacy, consumer fairness and breach exposure: the technology organization can increase usable AI capacity without creating a separate external data path for sensitive workloads.
cases: Run customer service, fraud detection, demand forecasting, personalization and event-operations agents inside Live Nation's own VPC and security boundary.
Increase usable accelerator capacity through scheduling, batching and routing.
Keep prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies under enterprise control.

echo.pull: As Live Nation increases the release cadence across ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications, the organization needs secure dependencies that move at the same speed. Echo's model is to patch and harden the underlying components before teams consume them, rather than asking every application owner to remediate the same CVEs independently. This could materially simplify the software supply chain for ticketing, payments, identity, venue, mobile and content applications, including agent and AI workloads. For the technology organization, the benefit is fewer inherited vulnerabilities, more consistent provenance and less conflict between developer velocity and the controls needed in an environment shaped by availability, fraud, privacy, consumer fairness and breach exposure.
cases: Standardize hardened dependencies across ticketing, payments, identity, venue, mobile and content applications.
Remove known vulnerabilities before they enter application and agent build pipelines.
Use FIPS-validated options where stronger assurance is required.

act.pull: Live Nation's cloud estate now includes highly elastic ticketing and event workloads across cloud and venue environments, with services and agents communicating across boundaries that are difficult to understand from identity or network controls alone. Act Security matters because it learns how applications are intended to communicate, compares that with actual behavior and guides teams in closing unnecessary paths. The agentless approach could help the technology organization create business-context perimeters around ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications without redesigning the network first. That is a direct fit for availability, fraud, privacy, consumer fairness and breach exposure, where one over-permissioned pathway can expand the blast radius of a compromise.
cases: Map intended and actual communication across highly elastic ticketing and event workloads across cloud and venue environments.
Create least-privilege perimeters around ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications and their data services.
Reduce lateral-movement risk without deploying another host agent.

clover.pull: For Live Nation, shipping faster only works if security can understand the roadmap at the same speed. Clover Security integrates into the design and planning stage, where ticketing engineers, venue teams, promoters, fraud analysts, customer-care staff and security teams are defining new ticketing, venue, fan, artist and event-operations capabilities. It can surface risks from the context already captured in tickets and documents, then return specific requirements before architecture and code harden around a weak assumption. The fit is strongest for ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications, where early decisions touch availability, fraud, privacy, consumer fairness and breach exposure and where a preventable redesign near release would slow the broader technology program.
cases: Review new ticketing, venue, fan, artist and event-operations capabilities while requirements and architecture are still being shaped.
Map security decisions back to tickets, design documents and accountable owners.
Reduce late-stage rework across ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications without adding another approval queue.

drata.pull: Live Nation has to prove trust across consumer protection, antitrust scrutiny, payments, privacy, breach response and accessibility while its technology estate and third-party relationships continue to change. Drata matters because it turns controls, evidence, risks and assurance signals into a continuously monitored trust graph rather than a periodic collection exercise. The technology organization could use it to connect policies and owners to the systems behind ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications, surface control drift and reuse evidence across overlapping reviews. That makes compliance more operational and gives leadership a current view of the risks that could slow progress toward reliable fan and venue experiences during the moments of highest demand.
cases: Continuously map controls and evidence across ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications and highly elastic ticketing and event workloads across cloud and venue environments.
Reuse assurance work across consumer protection, antitrust scrutiny, payments, privacy, breach response and accessibility instead of rebuilding it for every review.
Give leaders a current view of control drift, ownership and third-party risk.

orca.pull: Live Nation's cloud estate spans highly elastic ticketing and event workloads across cloud and venue environments, increasing the number of workloads, identities, data stores and AI services that security teams must understand together. Orca Security matters as an agentless CNAPP that unifies posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance context across AWS, Azure and GCP. It could give the technology organization immediate coverage without another deployment campaign, then prioritize the attack paths that place fan, event, ticket, inventory, payment, venue and fraud data or ticketing, venue, identity, payments, mobile, content and customer-service applications at real risk. That context is critical to reliable fan and venue experiences during the moments of highest demand because cloud scale makes raw alert volume an ineffective operating model.
cases: Gain agentless visibility across Live Nation's cloud estate and emerging AI workloads.
Prioritize attack paths into sensitive Live Nation data and critical applications.
Unify posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance remediation in one view.
